conditionA_Baseline.name: Capture and Register Loss Information
conditionA_Baseline.summary: A Clerk captures and registers loss information in the claims-capturing system, entering policy and incident details, while the System performs background validations and assigns a claim number. The Clerk confirms completion once sufficient information is provided.

steps.description: Begins loss entry in the claims-capturing system.
Enters available policy details.
Enters incident details.
Asynchronously validates entered data.
Matches the loss to a policy.
Checks for duplicate claims.
Assigns a claim number.
Retrieves related coverage information.
Supports adjuster assignment.
Confirms completion of data entry.
Stores the loss information.
Triggers acknowledgment actions.
Ensures the claim is ready for further processing.

loops.condition: While the Clerk is entering and revising details until confirmation of completion

conditionB_EnhancedHITL.summary: A Clerk captures and registers loss information in the claims-capturing system, entering policy and incident details, while the System performs background validations and assigns a claim number. The Clerk confirms completion once sufficient information is provided.

steps.description: Begins loss entry in the claims-capturing system.
Enters available policy details.
Enters incident details.
Asynchronously validates entered data.
Matches the loss to a policy.
Checks for duplicate claims.
Assigns a claim number.
Retrieves related coverage information.
Supports adjuster assignment.
Confirms completion of data entry.

flows.condition: If potential overlaps are found, and the Clerk decides the current entry is a true duplicate.

steps.description: Reviews the list of existing claims displayed by the System.
Decides the current entry is a true duplicate and opens the existing claim.

flows.condition: If potential overlaps are found, and the Clerk decides the current entry is a distinct claim.

steps.description: Reviews the list of existing claims displayed by the System.
Decides the current entry is a distinct claim, allowing them to proceed with the current entry.

flows.condition: If a submitted policy number is invalid ('Policy not found').

steps.description: Prompts the Clerk with error message 'Policy not found'.
Retries with different search details for an invalid policy number.

flows.condition: If a submitted name doesn't match ('mismatched').

steps.description: Prompts the Clerk with error message suggesting alternative policyholders or requiring re-entry.
Corrects the policy number or name for a mismatched policy holder.

flows.condition: If data submitted in Phase 1 (Policy Identification) is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields.

steps.description: Prompts the Clerk with error messages for incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields related to policy details.
Corrects the input before proceeding.

flows.condition: If validation in Phase 2 fails.

steps.description: Forces a re-evaluation or warns the Clerk when validation fails.
Corrects the invalid or incomplete information.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available during Phase 4, and the Clerk opts to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available during Phase 4, and the System automatically assigns a default adjuster.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster.

flows.condition: If the basic information entered in Phase 2 fails validation (e.g., it's incomplete or changed after a duplicate check), before a formal Claim Number is assigned.

steps.description: Forces a re-evaluation by the Clerk.
Corrects the invalid or incomplete information.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, and the Clerk opts to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, and the System automatically assigns a default adjuster.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster.

flows.condition: If the initial entry of basic loss information is invalid or incomplete during the duplicate check (e.g., if data changed after the check).

steps.description: Warns the Clerk that basic loss information is invalid or incomplete.
Re-evaluates or corrects the basic information before proceeding.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, and the Clerk opts to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk of the unavailability of suitable adjusters.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, and in specific timeout scenarios, the System automatically assigns a default adjuster.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk of the unavailability of suitable adjusters.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster in specific timeout scenarios.

flows.condition: If the basic information becomes invalid or incomplete after the duplicate check, the System would force a re-evaluation of the data or issue a warning to the Clerk.

steps.description: Forces a re-evaluation of the data or issues a warning to the Clerk.
Corrects the invalid information before proceeding with the claim.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available as determined by the System, and the Clerk opts to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available as determined by the System, and in some scenarios, the System may automatically assign a default adjuster if a specific timeout or business rule is met.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk that no suitable adjusters are available.
Automatically assigns a default adjuster if a specific timeout or business rule is met.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available as determined by the System, and the Clerk opts to save the claim for later assignment.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk of suitable adjusters unavailability.
Saves the claim for later assignment.

flows.condition: If no suitable adjusters are available as determined by the System, and the System automatically assigns a default adjuster, particularly in timeout scenarios, to prevent process blockage.

steps.description: Notifies the Clerk of suitable adjusters unavailability.
Assigns a default adjuster, particularly in timeout scenarios, to prevent process blockage.

flows.condition: If the final validation in Step 13 fails because minimum required information is missing, and the Clerk proceeds to provide the information.

steps.description: Warns the Clerk about the deficiencies when minimum required information is missing.
Prevents the Clerk from exiting the claim until the required information is provided.
Provides the required information.

flows.condition: If the final validation in Step 13 fails because minimum required information is missing, and the Clerk insists on exiting without completing the necessary fields.

steps.description: Warns the Clerk about the deficiencies when minimum required information is missing.
Discards unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting without completing the necessary fields.

loops.condition: While the Clerk is entering and revising details until confirmation of completion

questions: How specifically is "Asynchronously validates entered data." performed in step 4? What are the detailed actions?
How specifically is "Checks for duplicate claims." performed in step 6? What are the detailed actions?
What happens if the data submitted in step 2 is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields?
What happens if the validation in step 4 fails? (Asynchronously validates entered data.)
What happens if the data submitted in step 2 is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields?
What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 7?
answers: Step 4 in the detailed description is 'External Data Pull'. The phrasing 'Asynchronously validates entered data' is not directly from the provided process overview for any specific step. Assuming this refers to the 'System validates this entry' mentioned in Phase 2, this validation typically involves checking data types, format constraints (e.g., date formats, numeric ranges), mandatory field completion, and sometimes cross-referencing with master data tables to ensure data integrity. These checks are usually automated and provide immediate feedback to the Clerk.

Phase 2, 'Initial Loss Capture & Duplicate Check,' details this. The System performs a check for existing competing claims based on parameters like insured's name, policy number, date of loss, and type of incident. If potential overlaps are found, the System typically displays a list of these existing claims. The Clerk then reviews this list and decides whether the current entry is a true duplicate (in which case they open the existing claim) or a distinct claim (allowing them to proceed with the current entry).

If data submitted in Phase 1 (Policy Identification) is incomplete, invalid, or missing required fields, the System would likely prompt the Clerk with error messages. For instance, if a policy number is invalid, it may state 'Policy not found'. If a name doesn't match, it might suggest alternative policyholders or require the Clerk to re-enter the data. The Clerk must correct the input before proceeding, as stated by 'If not found: The Clerk must retry with different search details' or 'If mismatched: The Clerk must correct the policy number or name'.

The detailed description does not have a Step 4 focusing on 'Asynchronously validates entered data'. However, assuming this refers to the validation in Phase 2 ('Initial Loss Capture & Duplicate Check'), if validation fails, the System forces a re-evaluation or warns the Clerk. The Clerk would then need to correct the invalid or incomplete information before the System assigns a formal Claim Number and allows progression.

The detailed description for Phase 1 indicates that if the policy search results in 'not found' or 'mismatched' entries, the Clerk 'must retry with different search details' or 'must correct the policy number or name'. This means the system likely prevents progression until valid and complete policy identification data is provided. Similarly, the 'System validates this entry' in Phase 2 implies a halt until basic loss information is valid.

If no suitable adjusters are available during Phase 4 (Adjuster Assignment & Completion), the System notifies the Clerk. The Clerk has two options: they may save the claim for later assignment, or the System may automatically assign a default adjuster, particularly in scenarios where a timeout or critical response is needed. This allows the claim registration to complete while flagging it for subsequent adjuster review or reassignment.

questions: What happens if the validation in step 6 fails? (Checks for duplicate claims.)
What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 7?
answers: If the basic information entered in Phase 2 fails validation (e.g., it's incomplete or changed after a duplicate check), the System forces a re-evaluation by the Clerk. This ensures critical data is correct before proceeding. The Clerk must then correct the invalid/incomplete information before the System will proceed to assign a formal Claim Number.

If no suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, the System notifies the Clerk of this unavailability. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment, allowing them to proceed with other tasks and assign an adjuster when one becomes available. Alternatively, the System may assign a default adjuster, particularly in situations where a timely assignment is critical or if a timeout occurs without explicit Clerk intervention.

questions: What happens if the validation in step 6 fails? (Checks for duplicate claims.)
What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 7?
answers: If the initial entry of basic loss information is invalid or incomplete during the duplicate check (e.g., if data changed after the check), the System warns the Clerk. The Clerk must then re-evaluate or correct the basic information before proceeding to ensure data integrity.

If no suitable adjusters are available when the Clerk attempts to assign one, the System notifies the Clerk of this unavailability. The Clerk then has the option to either save the claim for later assignment or, in specific timeout scenarios, the System may automatically assign a default adjuster.

questions: What happens if the validation in step 6 fails? (Checks for duplicate claims.)
What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 7?
answers: If the basic information becomes invalid or incomplete after the duplicate check, the System would force a re-evaluation of the data or issue a warning to the Clerk. The Clerk would then need to correct the invalid information before proceeding with the claim, integrating back into the main flow after successful validation.

If no suitable adjusters are available as determined by the System, the System notifies the Clerk. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment, or in some scenarios, the System may automatically assign a default adjuster if a specific timeout or business rule is met.

questions: What happens if no resources (agents, slots, capacity) are available at step 7?
What happens if the validation in step 13 fails? (Ensures the claim is ready for further processing.)
answers: If no suitable adjusters are available, the System notifies the Clerk. The Clerk then has the option to save the claim for later assignment or the System may assign a default adjuster, particularly in timeout scenarios, to prevent process blockage.

If the final validation in Step 13 fails because minimum required information is missing, the System warns the Clerk about the deficiencies. The System prevents the Clerk from exiting the claim until the required information is provided, or it may discard unsaved changes if the Clerk insists on exiting without completing the necessary fields.

groundTruth.name: Register Loss
groundTruth.summary: Clerk captures loss information in claims system through flexible data entry, with system validation, duplicate detection, and automatic saving
postconditions: Loss information is captured and stored

steps.description: Enters insured's policy number or name and date of incident
Populates available policy information and indicates claim is matched to policy
Enters basic loss information
Confirms no existing competing claims and assigns claim number
Continues entering loss information specific to claim line
Has System pull other coverage information from other computer systems
Selects and assigns an adjuster
Confirms they are finished
Saves loss and triggers acknowledgement to be sent to agent

flows.condition: Power failure during loss capture

steps.description: Autosaves intermittently at transaction commit points

flows.condition: Claim is not for our company to handle

steps.description: Indicates claim is entered only for recording purposes and continues or ends loss

flows.condition: Found policy information does not match the insured's information

steps.description: Enters correct policy number or insured name and asks System to populate with new policy index information

flows.condition: Using search details, System could not find a policy

steps.description: Returns to loss and enters available data

flows.condition: Clerk changed policy number, date of loss or claim line after initial policy match

steps.description: Validates changes, populates loss with correct policy information, validates and indicates claim is matched to policy
Warns Clerk to re-evaluate coverage

flows.condition: System cannot validate policy match

steps.description: Warns Clerk
Finds the policy using the search details for policy

flows.condition: Clerk wants to restart a loss which has been interrupted, saved or needs completion

steps.description: Finds a loss using search details for loss
Opens it for editing

flows.condition: Clerk changes claim line previously entered and no line specific data has been entered

steps.description: Presents appropriate line specific sections of loss based on new claim line

flows.condition: Clerk changes claim line previously entered and there is data in some line specific fields

steps.description: Warns that data exists and asks Clerk to either cancel changes or proceed with new claim line

flows.condition: Clerk cancels change

steps.description: Continues with the loss

flows.condition: Clerk insists on new claim line

steps.description: Blanks out line specific data but keeps all basic claim level data

flows.condition: System detects possible duplicate claim

steps.description: Displays a list of possible duplicate claims from loss database
Selects and views a claim from the list

flows.condition: Clerk finds that the claim is a duplicate

steps.description: Opens duplicate claim from list for editing if not yet marked completed and may delete previously saved data

flows.condition: Clerk finds that the claim is not a duplicate

steps.description: Returns to loss and completes it

flows.condition: Preliminary loss information is changed after initial duplicate claim check is done

steps.description: Performs duplicate claim check again

flows.condition: Clerk needs to save loss before completion

steps.description: Has System save the loss for completion at a later time

flows.condition: Claim line or loss description are changed after coverage was reviewed

steps.description: Warns Clerk to re-evaluate coverage

flows.condition: Clerk confirms finished without completing minimum information

steps.description: Warns Clerk it cannot accept loss without date of loss, insured name or policy number and handling adjuster

flows.condition: Clerk decides to continue or save without marking complete

steps.description: Continues entering loss or saves without marking complete

flows.condition: Clerk insists on exiting without entering minimum information

steps.description: Discards any intermediate saves and exits

flows.condition: Missing required fields for claim number assignment

steps.description: Warns Clerk it cannot assign claim number without required fields and directs to fields requiring entry

flows.condition: Timeout: System decides to commit but handling adjuster not entered

steps.description: Assigns default adjuster
